params.heading: Republic of Korea – Working Holiday
params.anchor: Description: The Working Holiday category is designed for citizens of the Republic of Korea who intend to travel in Canada and who wish to find temporary paid employment to help pay for their trip (up to 24 months per participation).

params.heading: République de Corée — Vacances-travail
params.anchor: Description : La catégorie Vacances-travail est conçue pour les citoyens de la République de Corée qui envisagent de voyager au Canada et qui souhaitent trouver un emploi temporaire rémunéré pour les aider à payer leur voyage (jusqu’à 24 mois par participation).

params.heading: What is the tax-free threshold
params.anchor: The tax-free threshold is the amount of income you can earn before you pay tax. Most Australian residents can claim tax-free threshold on the first $18,200 of the income they earn in the income year.

params.anchor: Employers in federally regulated private sectors are required to adjust their payrolls to ensure their employees are paid at least $18.15 per hour starting April 1, 2026. Should the provincial or territorial minimum wage rate exceed the federal rate, federally regulated employers must pay their employees the higher of the two.
The minimum wage in B.C. is currently $18.25 per hour (as of June 1, 2026).

params.anchor: The IEC service standard is 56 days for applications with complete information and all required supporting documents. A re-entry ban means a person may not be granted further visas to return to Australia. This is also known as an exclusion period. This may last for up to 3 years although some people can be permanently excluded.
You must hold an eligible visa when you apply. You must have held that visa for at least 3 years before you apply.
